summary: Tested Design Requests module rename (marketing_requests -> 'Design Requests - OLD', design_requests_new -> 'Design Requests') + request-type default icon fallback on cards + lead-driven creation targeting NEW module. Backend fully working (RBAC migrated, icon upload/serve/enrich, from-lead endpoint, lead_id filter). Frontend nav labels, H1 headings, Kanban icon fallback, List view thumbnails and lead-initiated creation all pass. ONE CRITICAL UI BUG: the Edit Request Type dialog does not render the 'Default icon' section — admins cannot upload a type icon via UI.

ui_bugs.issue: The 'Default icon' section is completely missing from the dialog JSX. Handlers/state exist (onIconChange, form.icon_file_id, TypeIconThumb) but no <input type='file' data-testid='mr-type-icon-input'>, no upload button (mr-type-icon-upload-btn), no preview (mr-type-icon-preview) is rendered. Result: admins cannot upload a per-type default icon through the UI. Only Name / Design lead / Production lead / Active are shown. Backend PATCH /api/marketing-request-types/{id} { icon_file_id } works fine (verified via pytest and by uploading via API).

action_items: Add the missing 'Default icon' UI section to the Edit/Create Request Type dialog in /app/frontend/src/pages/MarketingRequestTypeMasters.js. It must include: (a) hidden , (b) a wrapper with data-testid='mr-type-icon-preview' showing either or a placeholder, (c) an 'Upload icon' button with data-testid='mr-type-icon-upload-btn' that triggers the hidden file input. Currently the section is entirely missing between the 'Production lead (days)' and 'Active' rows in the dialog (approximately after line 304 in MarketingRequestTypeMasters.js).

Fix the review-request/documented route path for Type Masters: the running app uses /admin/request-types (see App.js line 420 + DashboardLayout href), not /marketing-request-types. Either add a redirect or update docs.
Optional: expose a public /api/roles/module-labels endpoint (currently 404) so external tools can verify module labels without inspecting role responses.
critical_code_review_comments: MarketingRequestTypeMasters.js has plumbed all the icon state, uploader, save payload and thumbnail renderer, but is missing the dialog UI to actually pick a file. High-impact copy-paste omission.
TypeIconThumb hides itself onError (style.display='none'). Fine, but consider showing a small placeholder icon on failure so the row layout doesn't jump.

design_requests_new._enrich_requestor_city issues one extra Mongo query per list call to build icon_by_type; acceptable at current volume but could be memoized or joined with an $lookup pipeline for large tenants.

The 4 lead-driven action buttons (request-neck-tags-btn, create-bottle-design-btn, request-bottle-design-btn, request-bottle-sample-btn) all correctly hit /api/design-requests-new/from-lead/... — verified via network log.

Sidebar labels and route/H1 combinations are internally consistent: 'Design Requests' -> /design-requests-new -> H1 'Design Requests'; 'Design Requests - OLD' -> /marketing-requests -> H1 'Design Requests - OLD'.

Marketing Request Type icon file cascade fallback (own logo -> request_type_icon_url -> placeholder) verified on both Kanban and List views: kanban-card-image-* elements present for cards with icon OR logo (13 of 21), remaining 8 fall back to placeholder; list view has 40 req-thumb-* elements (20 rows x desktop table + mobile card) with either or

success_rate.frontend: 83% (5 of 6 review items pass; type-icon UI upload is broken)

seed_data_creation: Uploaded a 32x32 green PNG via /api/design-requests-new/upload and attached to request type 'Neck Tags' (id 8e6173f8-5490-4c6a-9950-ab0f0f029b47) via PATCH /api/marketing-request-types/{id}. Created 1 additional lead-driven design request DRN-2026-0003 for lead 08c93122-99fc-4587-b31c-559649f29c17 (Drive Folder Test Lead) — safe test data, can remain.
context_for_next_testing_agent:
PASS matrix: (1) Sidebar shows both 'Design Requests' and 'Design Requests - OLD' as CEO. (2) /design-requests-new renders H1 'Design Requests'; /marketing-requests renders H1 'Design Requests - OLD'. (3) Kanban board on /design-requests-new: cards without own logo but whose type has icon_file_id render pointing to /api/design-requests-new/files/{icon_file_id}; cards with own logo render own logo; both work. (4) List view on /design-requests-new: leading
contains if request has logo or type has icon, else an SVG tag placeholder. (5) Lead detail (id 08c93122-...): Customer branding card shows 4 buttons (request-neck-tags-btn, create-bottle-design-btn, request-bottle-design-btn, request-bottle-sample-btn); clicking Neck Tags posts to /api/design-requests-new/from-lead/{leadId}/neck-tags and the new DRN-* appears in the lead's requests list; clicking the newly created row navigates to /design-requests-new/{id} (NOT /marketing-requests/{id}); detail page lifecycle = 'Design Requests - New Lifecycle (default)'. (6) Backend pytest_iteration_289 all pass (see xml). FAIL: (F1) Type Masters at /admin/request-types (NOT /marketing-request-types as review request stated) — Edit dialog missing icon upload UI (mr-type-icon-preview / mr-type-icon-upload-btn / mr-type-icon-input NOT rendered). Once main agent adds the JSX, retest by opening the Edit dialog for Neck Tags, uploading a small PNG, saving, and verifying the type row shows the thumbnail (TypeIconThumb).
rca of the issue: In /app/frontend/src/pages/MarketingRequestTypeMasters.js the developer wired up all the plumbing: state (setUploadingIcon), form.icon_file_id, onIconChange handler (posts to /api/design-requests-new/upload), save includes icon_file_id in the payload, and TypeIconThumb renders the icon in the table row. However the JSX section that actually renders the file input + upload button + preview inside <DialogContent> is missing (the dialog contents jump from 'Production lead (days)' straight to 'Active' toggle). The reproduction is: login as CEO -> /admin/request-types -> click the pencil icon on any type -> the dialog opens with only Name/Design lead/Production lead/Active fields, and NO 'Default icon' section. Fix: add the missing JSX block referenced above between the lead-time grid and the Active row.
